mechanism: Potent FLT3 and AXL inhibitor
additional_notes: Approved for relapsed/refractory FLT3‑mutated AML; MOLM13 sensitive to FLT3 inhibition. Source did not provide assay concentrations.

mechanism: Selective FLT3 inhibitor with potency against FLT3‑ITD
additional_notes: Potent preclinical activity in FLT3‑ITD models. Source did not provide assay concentrations.

mechanism: Multi‑kinase inhibitor including FLT3
additional_notes: Approved in combination with chemotherapy for newly diagnosed FLT3‑mutant AML. Source did not provide assay concentrations.

mechanism: Multi‑kinase inhibitor (includes FLT3 among targets)
additional_notes: Used off‑label/clinical reports and in vitro studies show activity against FLT3‑mutant AML. Source did not provide assay concentrations.

mechanism: Multi‑kinase inhibitor with FLT3 activity
additional_notes: Preclinical activity against FLT3‑ITD and some resistant FLT3 mutants. Source did not provide assay concentrations.

mechanism: Selective BCL‑2 inhibitor (induces apoptosis)
additional_notes: Strong clinical activity in AML in combination regimens; many AML cell lines including FLT3‑mutant are sensitive. Source did not provide assay concentrations.

mechanism: CDK9/CDK inhibitor — transcriptional repression leading to MCL‑1 reduction
additional_notes: Also known as flavopiridol. Shows preclinical and clinical activity in AML and can synergize with BCL‑2 inhibitors. Source did not provide assay concentrations.

mechanism: Polo‑like kinase 1 (PLK1) inhibitor — impairs mitosis
additional_notes: Preclinical activity in AML; PLK1 is a validated mitotic target in AML. Source did not provide assay concentrations.

mechanism: Pan‑histone deacetylase inhibitor — alters chromatin and gene expression leading to cytotoxicity
additional_notes: HDAC inhibitors can sensitize AML cells to other agents. Source did not provide assay concentrations.

mechanism: Histone deacetylase (HDAC) inhibitor
additional_notes: Also known as SAHA. Explored in AML combination strategies. Source did not provide assay concentrations.

mechanism: Incorporation into DNA/RNA leading to DNA hypomethylation and reactivation of silenced genes
additional_notes: Standard therapies in AML and MDS; often used as backbone for combinations. Source did not provide assay concentrations.

mechanism: Thioredoxin reductase inhibitor — promotes redox stress (redox poison)
additional_notes: Reported preclinical AML cytotoxicity and early clinical interest in hematologic malignancies. Source did not provide assay concentrations.

mechanism: Multiple proposed mechanisms including aldehyde dehydrogenase inhibition and proteasome inhibition (enhanced with copper)
additional_notes: Often studied with copper to enhance anticancer effects; reported in vitro anticancer activity including leukemia models. Source did not provide assay concentrations.

mechanism: Acts as an uncoupler and can affect Wnt/STAT/mitochondrial pathways depending on context
additional_notes: Broad reported anticancer activity in preclinical studies; some AML models sensitive. Source did not provide assay concentrations.

mechanism: Binds microtubules — disrupts microtubule function leading to apoptosis
additional_notes: Repurposed as anticancer agent in preclinical leukemia models. Source did not provide assay concentrations.

mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial complex I and activates AMPK — metabolic modulation
additional_notes: Can impair leukemia cell survival and sensitize to other agents in vitro. Source did not provide assay concentrations.

mechanism: Inhibits HMG‑CoA reductase — disrupts mevalonate pathway and can promote proapoptotic effects
additional_notes: Listed as representative of statins; explored for antiproliferative effects in AML. Source did not provide assay concentrations.

mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial translation at higher concentrations relevant to mitochondrial ribosomes
additional_notes: Reported to target leukemia stem‑like compartments and mitochondrial function in AML models. Source did not provide assay concentrations.

mechanism: BTK (and related TEC family kinases) inhibitor
additional_notes: Some preclinical reports of activity in AML subsets and in combinations; variable single‑agent activity. Source did not provide assay concentrations.

mechanism: Selective JAK1 and JAK2 inhibitor
additional_notes: Activity in some myeloproliferative and AML contexts, particularly with JAK pathway activation or cooperating mutations. Source did not provide assay concentrations.
